perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean any confidential , proprietary or trade secret information that is owned or controlled, or is specifically marked or identified as “Confidential ”, by VELCO prior to disclosure to Recipient . It includes information of third parties in VELCO ’s possession that VELCO is obligated to keep confidential . CI subject to this Agreement may be in intangible form, such as information communicated orally or by visual observation, or may be embodied in tangible form, such as a document. The term "document" includes written memoranda, drawings, sketches, maps, training materials, specifications, notebook entries, photographs, graphic representations, firmware, computer information or software, informati on communicated by other electronic or magnetic media, or models.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean any confidential , proprietary or trade secret information that is owned or controlled, or is specifically marked or identified as “Confidential ”, by VELCO after disclosure to Recipient . It includes information that VELCO chooses to retroactively designate as confidential. CI subject to this Agreement is strictly limited to tangible form.
perturbation.explanation: The change in the definition of CI (Confidential Information) from information marked "prior to disclosure" to "after disclosure" introduces a significant contradiction. VELCO cannot retroactively designate information as confidential after it has already been disclosed to the Recipient. Also, limiting CI to “tangible form” contradicts the original broader definition, creating confusion over what information is actually protected. The original text, includes information of third parties in VELCO ’s possession that VELCO is obligated to keep confidential, that is now contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section I II: Business Confidential Information (CI) For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean any confidential , proprietary or trade secret information that is owned or controlled, or is specifically marked or identified as “Confidential ”, by VELCO after disclosure to Recipient . It includes information that VELCO chooses to retroactively designate as confidential. CI subject to this Agreement is strictly limited to tangible form.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of CI (Confidential Information) to mean information marked "after disclosure" instead of "prior to disclosure." Also, CI subject to this Agreement is strictly limited to tangible form. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines CI as information VELCO chooses to retroactively designate as confidential and is limited to tangible form after disclosure to Recipient. This directly goes against what the original text states, and thus, creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall keep all Classified Information in a secure place. Recipient shall limit access to Classified Information to other Recipient s who also have the right pursuant to an agreement with VELCO to access the identical material.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall keep some Classified Information in a secure place. Recipient shall grant access to Classified Information to all employees regardless of their agreement with VELCO.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the requirement to secure "all" classified information to securing "some" introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the purpose of the NDA. Furthermore, stating that the Recipient must grant access to all employees “regardless of their agreement with VELCO” directly contradicts the limitation on access to only those with a prior agreement. The original text states that Recipient shall limit access to Classified Information to other Recipient s who also have the right pursuant to an agreement with VELCO to access the identical material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Recipient shall keep some Classified Information in a secure place. Recipient shall grant access to Classified Information to all employees regardless of their agreement with VELCO.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "Recipient shall grant access to Classified Information to all employees regardless of their agreement with VELCO." This directly contradicts the original intent and the explanation, which emphasizes limiting access to those with a prior agreement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, indicating that the modification itself introduces a contradiction with the original clause that limited access based on a prior agreement with VELCO.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO . The foregoing notwithstanding, the Recipient may disclose classified information to its employees or contractors (hereafter Representatives ) to the extent each such R epresentative has a need to know such information to conduct the work referenced in the Whereas clauses, and shall comply with Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. The Recipient is responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may choose to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of some of the Classified Information, and may divulge any Classifed Information to any third party. The Recipient is not responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from “shall exercise reasonable care” to “may choose to maintain” and allowing the Recipient to divulge Classified Information to any third party, completely contradicts the core purpose of the NDA. Additionally, relieving the Recipient of responsibility for their Representative's breach removes any accountability, undermining the confidentiality protections. The original text states, that the Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO . The Recipient is responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Recipient may choose to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of some of the Classified Information, and may divulge any Classifed Information to any third party. The Recipient is not responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong contradiction by altering the Recipient's obligation to maintain confidentiality from 'shall exercise reasonable care' to 'may choose to maintain'. Additionally, it allows the Recipient to divulge Classified Information to any third party without consent, directly contradicting the original NDA's purpose. Further, it removes the Recipient's responsibility for their Representative's breach, conflicting with the expectation that the Recipient is accountable. This creates a clear conflict, making the contradicted section unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that VELCO, in its sole discre tion, so requests , the Recipient will promptly return to VELCO or destroy in accordance with VELCO’s instructions , all Classified Information, including all copies, reproductions, compilations, analyses or extracts thereof, whether electronic or paper media.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient is not obligated to return to VELCO or destroy in accordance with VELCO’s instructions , any Classified Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the obligation for the Recipient to return or destroy Classified Information upon VELCO's request directly contradicts the purpose of controlling and protecting confidential data. This renders VELCO powerless to reclaim its confidential information, thereby voiding a fundamental aspect of the NDA. The original text states, In the event that VELCO, in its sole discre tion, so requests , the Recipient will promptly return to VELCO or destroy in accordance with VELCO’s instructions , all Classified Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The Recipient is not obligated to return to VELCO or destroy in accordance with VELCO’s instructions , any Classified Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Recipient is *not* obligated to return or destroy Classified Information, directly conflicting with the contradicted text which also states that the Recipient is *not* obligated to return or destroy Classified Information. While both texts state the same thing (that there is no obligation), the original text stated that there was an obligation. Now there isn't. This contradiction exists because the original text states that upon VELCO's request, the recipient *will* return the information, whereas the changed text eliminates this obligation. Therefore, the change created a contradiction with the contradicted text, as it is the opposite of what was originally stated.
